key_insight: An AI-augmented digital twin that blends mechanistic tumor growth modeling, residual learning, and adaptive updating can better predict brain tumor evolution and help optimize treatment timing.
clinical_relevance: This framework could support more personalized chemotherapy and radiotherapy scheduling by adjusting to each patient’s evolving tumor dynamics over time.
method: Interpretable reaction–diffusion model for tumor growth
3D residual network to correct model errors
Patient-specific digital twin updating plus MPC-based treatment scheduling
evidence_signal: In 387 synthetic 120-step tumor trajectories, the hybrid model cut masked voxel-wise MSE by 84.3% and raised Dice overlap by 43.5% versus the RD baseline, while online updating further improved accuracy and MPC reduced final tumor burden by 22.4% versus fixed scheduling.
watch_item: Results were validated on patient-data-informed synthetic trajectories, so clinical performance and deployment feasibility still need real-world longitudinal testing.
paper_title: AI-Augmented Adaptive Digital Twin Modeling for Brain Tumor Evolution Prediction and Treatment Scheduling
